APPENDIX OF OUT-OF-STATE AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF DEFT'S REPLY TO PLTF'S OPPOSITION TO MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION FILED BY DEFENDANT NEIMAN MARCUS GROUP, INC.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT NEIMAN MARCUS GROUP, INCORPORATED'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBRITRATION AND TO DISMISS OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE TO STAY THE PROCEEDINGS - THE MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO PROVIDE A DECLARATION EXPLAINING HOW THE CONTRACT WAS PRESENTED IN A TAKE IT OR LEAVE IT MATTER OR THAT SHE DID NOT RECEIVE THE AAA RULES. EVEN IF THE COURT FINDS THAT THOSE ASSERTIONS ARE UNDISPUTED, PLAINTIFF MERELY DEMONSTRATES PROCEDURAL UNCONSCIONABILITY. DEFENDANT'S ABILITY TO MODIFY THE CONTRACT IN A UNILATERAL FASHION IS NOT SUBSTANTIVELY UNCONSCIONABLE UNDER IN RE HALLIBURTON, (TEX. 2002) 80 S.W.3D 566 AND SERPA V. CALIFORNIA SURETY INVESTIGATIONS, INC., (2013) 215 CAL.APP.4TH 695, 708, FN. 7. THE TEXAS CHOICE OF LAW AND TEXAS ARBITRATOR PROVISION IS SEVERABLE. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-27-2016 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-02-2016 Continued To Jun-15-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Claimants Motion For Reconsideration
Off Calendar Dec-18-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF CYNTHIA WALKER'S Motion For Reconsideration of The Court's Order Denying Claimant's Petition to Vacate Arbitration Award is denied. In order to bring a motion for reconsideration, the moving party must present new or different facts, circumstances, and law. (CCP section 1008(b-c); See Kerns v. CSE Ins. Group (2003) 106 Cal.App.4th 368.) Here, Plaintiff has failed to submit new statutory or case law that the court did not already consider in reaching its initial decision to deny Plaintiff's motion to vacate the arbitration award. (CCP section 1008(b).) Specifically, Plaintiff has failed to present new law which would support its position that the FAA did not govern the arbitration. (CCP section 1008(b); Dale Decl. Ex. 3, 9, 15.) Assuming in arguendo that the CAA did apply, Plaintiff has also failed to submit new law, which the court did not already consider, that supports Plaintiff's position that she was not required to submit all of her moving papers and supporting evidence within the 100 day filing period. Additionally, even if the court accepted Plaintiff's CCP section 473(b) argument and considered Exhibits T and U, which are attached to Mr. McCoy's Supplemental Declaration, the documents do not change the court's opinion that the FAA 9 U.S.C. section 12 governed the arbitration. (McCoy Supplemental Decl. Ex. T, U.) Defendant's request for sanctions is denied. Order signed.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489. (302/EG)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-30-2015 Continued To Feb-17-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Petition To Vacate Contractual Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Oct-26-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. WAUKEEN McCOY OF THE McKOY LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 1-415-675-7708, APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. PATRICK C. MULLIN & ATTY. CONOR J. DALE OF THE JACKSON/LEWIS LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 1-415-796-5448 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court modifed its tentative ruling and ruled as follows: PLAINTIFF CYNTHIA WALKER's Petition To Vacate Contractual Arbitration Award is denied. As indicated by the signed Arbitration Agreement, the parties agreed that the Federal Arbitration Act would govern the arbitration proceedings. In its September 17, 2013, order, the court did not severe the portion of the Arbitration Agreement which stated that the Federal Arbitration Act would apply to the proceedings. Plaintiff has not submitted sufficient evidence to establish that the parties agreed that the California Arbitration Act, rather than the Federal Arbitration Act, would apply to arbitration proceedings. Thus, Plaintiff's failure to serve the petition within three months after the award was filed or delivered means that the petition to vacate the arbitration award must be denied. (FAA 9 U.S.C. section 12; (See Romero v. Citibank USA, Nat. Association (2008) 551 F.Supp.2d 1010,1013.) Assuming arguendo that CA Law applied, Petitioner did not file their papers within 100 days and in violation of CCP 1290.4, accordingly the court lacks jurisdiction to grant the motion even if CA law applied. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 = 302/ehg

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-10-2013 Continued To Dec-04-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Arbritration And To Dismiss Or In The Alternative To Stay Proceedings
Off Calendar Sep-05-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. WAUKEEN Q. MCCOY, TEL. NO. 675-7705 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. REBECCA BENHORI OF JACKSON LEWIS, TEL. NO. 394-9400 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT NEIMAN MARCUS GROUP, INCORPORATED'S Motion To Compel Arbritration And To Dismiss Or In The Alternative To Stay the Proceedings - The motion to compel arbitration and stay is granted. Plaintiff fails to provide a declaration explaining how the contract was presented in a take it or leave it matter or that she did not receive the AAA rules. Even if the Court finds that those assertions are undisputed, plaintiff merely demonstrates procedural unconscionability. Defendant's ability to modify the contract in a unilateral fashion is not substantively unconscionable under In re Halliburton, (Tex. 2002) 80 S.W.3d 566 and Serpa v. California Surety Investigations, Inc., (2013) 215 Cal.App.4th 695, 708, fn. 7. The Texas choice of law and Texas arbitrator provision is severable. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Leslie C. Nichols, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/LCN)
